target: SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275 paragraph — ISS-1808 verification (BOU-2017 single-station vs ≥3-station Tier 1 rule)
verification_result: ALREADY-FIXED — fix landed in applied-patches/suggested-patches-2026-04-30T17-20.json patch 1 (paired with the patch 0 line→block fix). Curmudgeon Hole 2 recommended option (a) — cite the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble — and that is exactly what landed. The current sections.json part4 paragraph now reads: "BOU 2017's −10.9 nT signal already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with the August 2017 eclipse contact times (the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble cited above — 207 observations across 39 total eclipses, including August 2017 — documents the same ≥3-station coherent Chapman-pattern, satisfying the rule's own multi-station threshold)." The parenthetical pre-empts the defender retreat that single-station BOU-2017 cannot satisfy the rule.

evidence.rationale_excerpt: Adding parenthetical referencing Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (already cited two paragraphs earlier) pre-empts the defender retreat that single-station evidence doesn't satisfy the rule.

holes_addressed: SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5 Hole 3 (UNCITED EMPIRICAL CLAIM, minor — absorbed by Hole 2 fix per curmudgeon's own note)
kernel_of_truth: The defender retreat was real: BOU-2017 alone really is one station, and the Tier 1 rule really does say ≥3 stations with r>0.5. Citing the Meza et al. 2021 ScienceDirect-published 207-observation INTERMAGNET ensemble (already in the section two paragraphs earlier) closes that retreat by showing the multi-station coherence is the well-documented Chapman signature, not a one-station outlier. The dome's Tier 1 multi-station threshold is met by 39 total eclipses worth of multi-station data — it just isn't met by zero multi-station ECM data, which the dome doesn't have.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why option (a) and not option (b) reframing as self-defeating?
anticipated_objections.response: Option (a) was the cheaper, lower-risk fix: cite the existing INTERMAGNET ensemble that's already in the section. Option (b) — calling the rule self-defeating because no single eclipse station can satisfy ≥3 stations — would be true but stronger than warranted; the rule is satisfiable by a multi-station deployment, which is how the dome intends to test it in August 2026. The fix as landed pre-empts the retreat without overclaiming.

anticipated_objections.objection: Does this fix create the same kind of literal misread Hole 1 fixed?
anticipated_objections.response: No — the parenthetical cites "the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble cited above" which back-references the same Meza et al. 2021 (arXiv:2107.12327) ScienceDirect link two paragraphs up. Reproducible: any reader can verify the back-pointer resolves.

summary_for_decider: ISS-1808 verification: fix already integrated 2026-04-30 (paired with ISS-1807 patch). Recommend CLOSE with fix_details pointing at applied-patches/suggested-patches-2026-04-30T17-20.json patch 1. No new prose needed.
recommended_close_fix_details: Fix landed 2026-04-30 in decider applied patch 1 of suggested-patches-2026-04-30T17-20.json: added parenthetical "(the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble cited above — 207 observations across 39 total eclipses, including August 2017 — documents the same ≥3-station coherent Chapman-pattern, satisfying the rule's own multi-station threshold)" to sections.json part4. Pre-empts the defender retreat that single-station BOU-2017 cannot satisfy the dome's Tier 1 ≥3-station rule. Verified 2026-05-11.
